translation_text: The grandest forms of active force
From Dao come, their only source.
Who can of Dao the nature tell? Our sight it flies, our touch as well.
Eluding sight, eluding touch, The forms of things all in it crouch; Eluding touch, eluding sight,
There are their semblances, all right.
Profound it is, dark and obscure;
Of what, when seen, shall then be told.
Now it is so; 'twas so of old.
Its name - what passes not away;
Things form and never know decay.

chinese_text: 吾何以知眾甫之狀哉？以此。
translation_text: How know I that it is so with all the beauties of existing things? By this (nature of the Dao).
